concepts.name: Vector
concepts.short_definition: A quantity having direction as well as magnitude, especially as determining the position of one point in space relative to another.

concepts.name: Dot Product
concepts.short_definition: A product of two vectors that results in a scalar quantity.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Vectors
concepts.short_definition: Two vectors whose dot product is zero.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Set
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors where every pair of distinct vectors is orthogonal.

concepts.name: Orthonormal Set
concepts.short_definition: An orthogonal set of vectors where each vector has a magnitude of one.

concepts.name: Vector Magnitude
concepts.short_definition: The length of a vector.

concepts.name: Unit Vector
concepts.short_definition: A vector with a magnitude of one.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Complement
concepts.short_definition: The set of all vectors orthogonal to every vector in a given subspace.

concepts.name: Subspace
concepts.short_definition: A subset of a vector space that is itself a vector space.

concepts.name: Projection
concepts.short_definition: The component of one vector that lies in the direction of another vector.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Projection
concepts.short_definition: The projection of a vector onto a subspace, such that the difference between the vector and its projection is orthogonal to the subspace.

concepts.name: Gram-Schmidt Process
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for orthogonalizing a set of vectors in an inner product space.

concepts.name: Linear Independence
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors where none can be written as a linear combination of the others.

concepts.name: Linear Combination
concepts.short_definition: A sum of vectors, each multiplied by a scalar.

concepts.name: Basis
concepts.short_definition: A set of linearly independent vectors that span a vector space.

concepts.name: Span
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible linear combinations of a set of vectors.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Basis
concepts.short_definition: A basis for a vector space where all vectors in the basis are mutually orthogonal.

concepts.name: Orthonormal Basis
concepts.short_definition: A basis for a vector space where all vectors are orthonormal.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A square matrix whose columns (and rows) form an orthonormal set.

concepts.name: Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or expressions, arranged in rows and columns.

concepts.name: Transpose
concepts.short_definition: A matrix obtained by interchanging the rows and columns of a given matrix.

concepts.name: Identity Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A square matrix with ones on the main diagonal and zeros elsewhere.

concepts.name: Inverse Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A matrix that, when multiplied by another matrix, yields the identity matrix.

concepts.name: Symmetric Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A square matrix that is equal to its transpose.

concepts.name: Positive Definite Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A symmetric matrix where all its eigenvalues are positive.

concepts.name: Eigenvalue
concepts.short_definition: A scalar associated with a linear transformation that describes how a corresponding eigenvector is scaled.

concepts.name: Eigenvector
concepts.short_definition: A non-zero vector that, when a linear transformation is applied to it, changes only by a scalar factor.

concepts.name: Linear Transformation
concepts.short_definition: A function between two vector spaces that preserves the operations of vector addition and scalar multiplication.

concepts.name: Scalar Multiplication
concepts.short_definition: Multiplying a vector by a scalar, which scales the vector's magnitude.

concepts.name: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
concepts.short_definition: An inequality relating the dot product of two vectors to their magnitudes.

concepts.name: Triangle Inequality
concepts.short_definition: An inequality stating that the sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than or equal to the length of the third side.

concepts.name: Inner Product Space
concepts.short_definition: A vector space equipped with an inner product.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Decomposition
concepts.short_definition: Expressing a vector as a sum of orthogonal components, often with respect to a subspace.

concepts.name: Least Squares Approximation
concepts.short_definition: Finding the vector in a subspace that is closest to a given vector, minimizing the squared error.

concepts.name: Normal Equations
concepts.short_definition: A system of linear equations used to find the least squares solution.

concepts.name: QR Decomposition
concepts.short_definition: Decomposing a matrix into the product of an orthogonal matrix and an upper triangular matrix.

concepts.name: Upper Triangular Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A square matrix where all the entries below the main diagonal are zero.

concepts.name: Singular Value Decomposition
concepts.short_definition: A factorization of a real or complex matrix into three other matrices, which have special properties.

concepts.name: Diagonal Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A square matrix where all the entries outside the main diagonal are zero.
